Fern Polrrlcal PRACTICEs C ouvtlsSIoN
428 J Street . Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
August 11,2006
Paul Melocoton
2080 Market Street, Suite 200
Riverside, California 92501
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-06-146
Dear Mr. Melocoton:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the post-
governmental employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'oAct").1 Since
you have not provided any facts related to a specific appearance before or cofirmunication
with your previous government agency employer, we are treating your request as one for
informal assistance.2 This letter should not be construed as assistance on any conduct
thatmay have already taken place. In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented.
The Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission") does not act as a finder of fact
when it renders assistance. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)
QUESTIONS
1. As a former employee of the California Department of Transportation
("Caltrans"), what limitations does the Act impose on you while working for CH2M Hill
on projects with the Riverside County Transportation Commission and the County of
Riverside?
2. Under the provisions of the Act's one-year ban, what constifutes "influencing
decisions" and do the restrictions prevent you from directly interacting with other
Caltrans districts besides District 8?
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c), copy enclosed.)


File No. I-06-146
Page No. 2
3. Does the one-year ban mean that you cannot have any contact with Caltrans?
Can you work "behind the scenes" for CH2M HILL and their clients on transportation
projects involving Caltrans as long as you do not directly interact with Caltrans?
CONCLUSIONS
1. As a former state government employee, you are subject to the Act's post-
goveflrmental employment restriction known as the permanent ban on "switching sides."
Additionally, if yourposition with Caltrans, as an Associate Environmental Planner,
should have been designated in Caltrans' conflict of interest code, you are also subject to
the one-yearban. Both the permanent ban and the one-year ban are outlined below.
2. An appearance or coflrlnunication is "for the purpose of influencing" if it is
made for the principal purpose of supporting, promoting, influencing, modifring,
opposing, delaying, or advancing the action or proceeding. The restrictions apply to
contacts made with all Caltrans districts and employees.
3. The one-year ban applies to any contacts with Caltrans or Caltrans employees
if made for the purpose of influencing administrative or legislative action, or influencing
any action or proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of
a permit, license, ffM! or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property.
However, you may work "behind the scenes" for CH2M HILL and their clients on
transportation projects involving Caltrans so long as you do not appear before or
communicate with Caltrans or its ernployees, and the proceeding is not one that would be
subject to the restrictions of the permanent ban, as discussed below.
FACTS
On May 22,2006, you accepted a position with CH2M HILL, as an 'Associate
Planner," and, accordingly, left your state position with Caltrans, as an'Associate
Environmental Planner - Environmental Planning Division." Currently, Caltrans does
not consider your former position a designated position under its conflict of interest code.
While working for Caltrans, you were primarily responsible for ensuring that state
transportation projects complied with all applicable state and federal environmental laws
and policies. Your duties also included the preparation of environmental documents
pursuant to the National Environmental PolicyAct and the California Environmental
Quality Act. The projects assigned to you were located within San Bemardino County.
Currently, CH2M HILL has existing contracts with both the Riverside County
Transportation Commission and the County of Riverside for transportation improvement
projects on state highways. These projects will ultimately require Caltrans' review and
approval.


File No. I-06-146
Page No. 3
ANALYSIS
Public officials who leave state service are subject to two types ofpost-
govemmental employment provisions under the Act,3 colloquially known as the
"revolving door" prohibitions.o We look to these provisions to answer your questions.
Permanent Ban: The first restriction is the "permanent ban" prohibiting a former
state employee from "switching sides" and participating, for compensation, in any
specific proceeding involving the State of California or assisting others in the
proceeding if the proceeding is one in which the former state employee
patticipated while employed by the state (see sections 8740I-87402, regulation
t874t.t).
The permanent ban is a lifetime ban and applies to any judicial, quasi-judicial, or
other proceeding in which you participated while you served as a state administrative
official. "'Judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding' means any proceeding,
application, request for a ruling or other determination, contract, claim, controversy,
investigation, charge, accusation, atrest or other particular matter involving u rp""ifi"
patty or parties in any court or state administrative agency . . . ." (Section 87a00(c).)
Additionally, * official is considered to have "participated" in a proceeding if he or she
took part in the proceeding "personally, and substantially through decision, approval,
disapproval, formal written recommendation, rendering advice on a substantial basis,
investigation, or use of confidential information . . . . (section 87a00(d).)
"The permanent ban does not apply to a 'new' proceeding even in cases where the
new proceeding is related to or grows out of a prior proceeding in which the official had
participated. A 'new' proceeding not subject to the permanent ban typically involves
different parties, a different subject matter, or different factual issues from those
considered in previous proceedings." (Rls/ Advice Letter, No. A-04-1g7; see also
Donovan Advice Letter, No. I-03-119.)
You have not provided any inforrnation as to your participation in any proceeding
that may affect your ability to engage in any of the conduct listed herein. To apply the
permanent ban to your situation, you need to determine if any of the actions in which you
now wish to engage, on behalf of your new employer, involves a proceeding in which
you participated, while employed at caltrans. (Regulation 1874L 1(a)(a).)
3 In addition, section 87407 prohttbits certain state and local officials from making, participating in
making, or using their official position to influence decisions affecting persons with whomthey are
negotiating employment, or has a\y affaugement conceming employment. (Section 874}7;regulation
18747.) a A comprehensive discussion of the elements of the two laws is set forth in the attached ool.eaving
Your State Job? Post-Employment Restrictions May Affect You." We have enclosed this document for
your information. we do not repeat this information in the body of this letter.


File No. I-06-146
Page No. 4
o One-Year Ban: The second restriction is the "one-year ban" prohibiting a state
employee from communicating, for compensation, with his o, h", former agency
for the purpose of influencing certain administrative or legislative action or
infl uencing certain proceedings. (See section 87 406, reguiation I g7 46.1 .)
The one-year ban applies to employees who are designated in their former
agency's conflict of interest code. However, the one-year ban also applies to employees
who made ot participated in the making of governmental decisions that had areasonably
foreseeable material effect on any financial interest. In other words, the one-year ban
applies to former employees who were or who should have been deiignatedin their
former employer's conflict of interest code. (Section g7406(dX1); ,"!rlutio,
t87a6r@)Q).)
A state employee "makes a goveflrmental decision" when actingwithin the scope
of his or her authority, he or she: 1) votes on a matter;2) appoints a perion; 3) obligates
his or her agency to any course of action; 4) enters into any contractual agreement on
belralf of his or her agency; or 5) determines not to act,uri"s, the determlnation is made
due to a conflict of interest. (Regulation rg702.l (copy enclosed).)
Additionally, a state employee "partrcipates in making a goveflrmental decision,,
when he or she negotiates (without significant substantive r"rri"*; with a government
entity or private person regarding a govemmental decision, or when he or-she advises or
makes recolnmendations to the decisionmaker (either directly or without significant
substantive review), by conducting research or an investigatiron or by pr"r#irrg ury
report, analysis or opinion, which requires the exercis" ofird"p"raeni3uagment on the
part of the employee and the outcome or putpose of which is to influence tle decision.
(Regulation I 87 02.2 (copy enclosed).)
We have advised that an employee participates in the making of a governmental
decision, even if it is reviewed by several of his oi her superiors, if any ofTtre following
apply: 1) the superiors rely on the data or analysis p."p*"d by the .-ploy"" without
checking it independently; 2) the superiors rely on the professional judgment of the
ernployee; or 3) the employee in some way actually influences the final decision.
(Lilyquist Advice Letter, No. M-96-31g [Memorandum to the Attomey General].)
Based on our understanding of Caltrans' current conflict of interest code, your
former Associate Environmental Planner position is not considered a designated iosition.
However, you have indicated that your position with Caltrans involved ensuring
compliance with state and federal laws and preparing environmental reports. The duties
you describe suggest that your supervisors may have relied on yol]r uruly.is without
independently checking, that your supervisors may have relied on your professional
judgment, or that your work may have influenced final decisions in .o-^" *uy.
AdditionallS we recently reviewed a similar revolving door question related to
Caltrans' conflict of interest code in the Wtlliams Advice Letter, No.l-OO-05g. In this


File No. I-06-146
Page No. 5
letter, we also found strong indications that asimilar position, "Associate Environmental
ll.u*"|- fatural Sciences Division" should have been designated in Caltrans, conflict of
rnterest code.
Moreover, nothing in your facts indicate that Caltrans has made a determination
that your position was properly not designated in its conflict of interest code.
AccordinglL there are strong indications that your position as an Associate
Environmental Planner should have been designated in Caltrans' conflict of interest code,
and that you are a public official subject to the one-year ban.5 rf vo"r porition with
Caltrans should have been_designatei, the one-ye uriuiwould apply fir Dmonths from
the date you left state employment. (section ai+0a616;; regulation tg746.t @;@;;-'-
The one-yearban applies to appearances or comrnunications for which you are
compensated for and which are made on behalf of anyperson as an agent, attorney, or
representative. (S_ection87406(d)(1); regulation 18746.1(b)(3) ana (i).) F;;* the facts
provided, you will beworking as an Associate Planner for CH2MHTLL. Since you will
be a paid employee of CH2M HILL, you will be working for compensation and in
representation of another person.
Appearances and communications are prohibited only if they are made for the
putpose of influencing an, "administrative or legislative action" or idiscret ionary act
involving the issuance, amendment, awardirrg, or revocation of a permit, license, grant, or
contract, or the sale orpurchase of goods orproperty.,, (sectionv+0a61(l); regulation
18746.1(bx5).) Regulation 18746.2 helps to- define-..purpose of influenc iig;: ay
providing that an appearance or communication is foittre pu{pose of influencing if it is
made for the principal pu{pose of supporting, promoting, influen"iog, -odiSrrg,
opposing, delaying, or advancing the action oiproceeding.
As the last step of the analysis, appearances and communications are prohibited if
they are before a state agency the public officiut worked for or represented or a state
agency which budget, personnel, and other operations are subjecf to the control of a state
agency the public official worked for or represented. (Section 87406(d)(rtr"grrtu,io,
18746.1(b)(6).) Since you are leaving a position with baltrans, the o;"-y"; applies to
any appearance or communications before Caltrans including all Caltrans districts and
employees.
We point out, however,thatnot all communications are prohibited by the one-
year ban' Appearances or communications before a former state agency employer, made
as part of "services performed to administer, implement, or fulfill tIr" r"q"ir*ents of an
5 We note that Caltrans is in the process of updating its conflict of interest code. As your position
with caltrans may have involved duties that would. indicate tlat the porition rioura i""" u"", arsignated
in Caltans' conflict of interest code, we must caution that unless you can provide documentation from
caltrans that-your position was properly not designated, it is advisable the you abide by the one-year ban.
o We have advisedthat the one-year ban commences when the employee i, oo lorg", under an
employment agreement and is no longer receiving compensation, including compensatior, fo?ltrrr"d,,
vacation time. (lteil Advice Letter, No. A-91-241)


File No. I-06-146
Page No. 6
existing permit, license, grant, contract, or sale agreement may be excluded from the
[one-year] prohibitions . . . provided the services do not involve the issuance,
amendment, awarding, or revocation of any of these actions or proceedings.,;
(Regulation 18746.1(b)(s)(A); euiring Advice Letter,No. A-0:-2 72; HaianAdvice
Letter, No. I-00-209.)
Additionally, appearances or communications are not restricted under the one-
yearban, if an individual:
"(1) Participates as a panelist or formar speaker at a conference or
similar public event for educational purposes or to disseminate
research and the subject matter does not pertain to a specific action
or proceeding;
(2) Attends a general informational meeting, seminar, or similar
event;
(3) Requests information conceming anyrnatter of public record;
(4) communicates with the press." (Regulation rg746.2(b)(r)-(4).)
Finally, the Commission has advised that aformer agency official may draft
proposals on a client's behalf to be submitted to the agency so long as the former
employee is not identified in connection with the client's "ffo.tr tJinfluence
administrative action. (CookAdvice Letter, No. 4-95-3 2l; Harrison Adviceletter, No.
A-92-289.) Similarly, a former agency official may use his or her expertise to advise
clients on the procedural requirements, plans, o. poli"i"r of the official,s former agency
so long as the employee is not identified with theemployer's efforts to influence the
agency. (Perry Advice Letter,No. 4-94-004.)
If you have other questions on this matter,please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
or
Enclosures

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. As a former employee of the California Department of Transportation
("Caltrans"), what limitations does the Act impose on you while working for CH2M Hill
on projects with the Riverside County Transportation Commission and the County of
Riverside?
2. Under the provisions of the Act's one-year ban, what constifutes "influencing
decisions" and do the restrictions prevent you from directly interacting with other
Caltrans districts besides District 8?
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c), copy enclosed.)
3. Does the one-year ban mean that you cannot have any contact with Caltrans?
Can you work "behind the scenes" for CH2M HILL and their clients on transportation
projects involving Caltrans as long as you do not directly interact with Caltrans?

CONCLUSION: 1. As a former state government employee, you are subject to the Act's post-
goveflrmental employment restriction known as the permanent ban on "switching sides."
Additionally, if yourposition with Caltrans, as an Associate Environmental Planner,
should have been designated in Caltrans' conflict of interest code, you are also subject to
the one-yearban. Both the permanent ban and the one-year ban are outlined below.
2. An appearance or coflrlnunication is "for the purpose of influencing" if it is
made for the principal purpose of supporting, promoting, influencing, modifring,
opposing, delaying, or advancing the action or proceeding. The restrictions apply to
contacts made with all Caltrans districts and employees.
3. The one-year ban applies to any contacts with Caltrans or Caltrans employees
if made for the purpose of influencing administrative or legislative action, or influencing
any action or proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of
a permit, license, ffM! or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property.
However, you may work "behind the scenes" for CH2M HILL and their clients on
transportation projects involving Caltrans so long as you do not appear before or
communicate with Caltrans or its ernployees, and the proceeding is not one that would be
subject to the restrictions of the permanent ban, as discussed below.

Fax (916) 322-0886 August 11,2006 Paul Melocoton 2080 Market Street, Suite 200 Riverside, California 92501 RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. I-06-146 Dear Mr. Melocoton: This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the post- governmental employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'oAct").1 Since you have not provided any facts related to a specific appearance before or cofirmunication with your previous government agency employer, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2 This letter should not be construed as assistance on any conduct thatmay have already taken place. In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) QUESTIONS 1. As a former employee of the California Department of Transportation ("Caltrans"), what limitations does the Act impose on you while working for CH2M Hill on projects with the Riverside County Transportation Commission and the County of Riverside? 2. Under the provisions of the Act's one-year ban, what constifutes "influencing decisions" and do the restrictions prevent you from directly interacting with other Caltrans districts besides District 8? I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations. 2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c), copy enclosed.) File No. I-06-146 Page No. 2 3. Does the one-year ban mean that you cannot have any contact with Caltrans? Can you work "behind the scenes" for CH2M HILL and their clients on transportation projects involving Caltrans as long as you do not directly interact with Caltrans? CONCLUSIONS 1. As a former state government employee, you are subject to the Act's post- goveflrmental employment restriction known as the permanent ban on "switching sides." Additionally, if yourposition with Caltrans, as an Associate Environmental Planner, should have been designated in Caltrans' conflict of interest code, you are also subject to the one-yearban. Both the permanent ban and the one-year ban are outlined below. 2. An appearance or coflrlnunication is "for the purpose of influencing" if it is made for the principal purpose of supporting, promoting, influencing, modifring, opposing, delaying, or advancing the action or proceeding. The restrictions apply to contacts made with all Caltrans districts and employees. 3. The one-year ban applies to any contacts with Caltrans or Caltrans employees if made for the purpose of influencing administrative or legislative action, or influencing any action or proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license, ffM! or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property. However, you may work "behind the scenes" for CH2M HILL and their clients on transportation projects involving Caltrans so long as you do not appear before or
